title: Upsert with NULL-safe required fields
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.bulk_upsert_users/1` that takes a list of complete user maps (with all NOT NULL fields — email, name) and upserts them. On conflict (email), replace `[:name, :updated_at]` only. Include timestamps (`inserted_at`, `updated_at`) in each row. Use `conflict_target: :email`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Must know NOT NULL must be supplied for every row (PostgreSQL validates INSERT before ON CONFLICT) AND use targeted replace.
research_citation: benzene73 Elixir Forum: 'PostgreSQL requires all NOT NULL columns to be provided in an INSERT, even if the row already exists. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING does NOT bypass this.'
